brief_summary: Layered four-row diagram, one row per independent design axis a split/ergo board can choose: monoblock vs split, row-stagger vs column-stagger, flat vs tented, flat plate vs sculpted well. Each row shows a small line-drawn icon pair with the more committing option marked by an amber dot, matching the axis descriptions in the article's opening section.
data_sources: split-ergo-buyers-guide.mdx § 'The four axes nobody separates' — 'Split versus monoblock... Row-stagger versus column-stagger... Flat versus tented... Flat plate versus sculpted well'

split-ergo-buyers-guide.mdx § 'The four axes nobody separates' — 'Column-stagger is the layout choice that produces the most immediate feel of "this is different" and also the layout choice with the steepest relearning curve'
split-ergo-buyers-guide.mdx § 'The four axes nobody separates' — 'Sculpted wells are the most committing choice on this list'
